tactical_shape: Ancelotti initially used Roger Ibañez at right-back, which left Brazil exposed and was targeted by Morocco.
After half-time, Ancelotti made tactical tweaks and replaced Casemiro and Ibañez with Fabinho and Danilo, producing a more structured system that pushed Morocco back into their own half.
standout_players: Vinícius Júnior rescued Brazil with the equaliser, scoring from a tight angle after creating space down the left.
Alisson was beaten by Saibari for Morocco’s goal and then almost made a costly late fumble.
Lucas Paquetá came close with a volley that Bounou tipped wide.
Igor Thiago missed a header in the first half and was later denied from a tight angle.
Casemiro struggled in midfield and was booked before being substituted.
Roger Ibañez was exposed at right-back and also booked.
fatigue_injuries: Neymar was sidelined by a calf injury.
Casemiro and Ibañez were both withdrawn at half-time after struggling and being booked.
home_read.momentum: Brazil were lacklustre and anxious early, but improved after adjustments; the result underlines that Ancelotti has plenty of work to do before they look like real contenders.

tactical_shape: Morocco pressed aggressively from the start and targeted Brazil’s improvised right-back area.
They played with a midfield anchored by Ayyoub Bouaddi and were able to push Brazil back in the first half.
In the second half the rhythm dropped and Ouahbi’s triple substitution suggested Morocco were prepared to settle for a point.
standout_players: Ayyoub Bouaddi produced an outstanding, assured midfield display beyond his 18 years.
Ismael Saibari scored a brilliant opening goal with a composed lob.
Brahim Díaz produced a sensational pass to set up the opener.
Achraf Hakimi created danger and could have done more when he shot instead of passing to Díaz.
Yassine Bounou made key saves from Paquetá, Igor Thiago and Luis Henrique.
Noussair Mazraoui drove forward from left-back and Neil El Aynaoui had an early shot blocked.
away_read.momentum: Morocco looked deservedly competitive and capable of going deep again, though the second-half drop in rhythm meant they had to settle for a draw.
